channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Veja os próximos passos após Lula indicar Jorge Messias ao STF
item.description: Lula nominated Jorge Messias to the STF following the retirement of Luís Roberto Barroso. The appointment requires Senate approval: first in the CCJ with a hearing and secret vote, then in the plenary, where an absolute majority (41 votes) is needed. Despite prior resistance, current political conditions favor confirmation.

item.title: Chega ao Senado mensagem presidencial indicando Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: President Lula nominated Jorge Messias for the Supreme Federal Court (STF), replacing Luís Roberto Barroso, and the nomination was sent to the Senate, where it must undergo a hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) and be approved by secret ballot with an absolute majority (41 senators). Messias, 45, has been Attorney General of the Union since 2023, with a career in public law and roles in the Dilma Rousseff government. Since 1894, no STF nominee has been rejected by the Senate.

item.title: Mendonça Filho deixa União Brasil após 40 anos para se filiar ao PL
item.description: Federal Deputy Mendonça Filho (PE) left União Brasil after over 40 years of affiliation to join the PL, the party of former President Jair Bolsonaro. His departure follows the formalization of the federation between União Brasil and PP, a move he had been considering since 2022. Mendonça Filho served as Minister of Education in the Temer government (2016-2018).

item.title: Valdemar Costa Neto elogia Messias e diz que Senado aprova indicação
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL, praised the nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF, stating that although he is aligned with the PT, he is a competent choice and that the Senate will approve it due to the government majority. He said he will not influence PL senators, whose majority opposes the nomination, but acknowledged approval is certain. The formal nomination followed bureaucratic delays and resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who preferred Rodrigo Pacheco for the vacancy.

item.title: PL dá dois meses para empresário viabilizar candidatura ao governo de MG
item.description: The PL gave two months to Fiemg president Flávio Roscoe to viable his candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais after his affiliation with the party on March 31. The goal is for him to travel across the state, increase his visibility, and demonstrate his capacity to run in the election, amid party concerns over Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential ticket in the country's second-largest electoral college.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro perdido no mundo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro made his international debut at CPAC in Dallas, promoting a conservative agenda that frames the world as a battle between conservatism and 'globalism,' which he blames for undermining traditional family values. He positioned himself as the political heir of his father, Jair Bolsonaro, echoing rhetoric previously advanced in 2019 by former Foreign Minister Ernesto Araújo, whose foreign policy was widely ridiculed.

item.title: André de Paula toma posse do Ministério da Agricultura
item.description: André de Paula (PSD) was sworn in as Minister of Agriculture on April 1, 2026, replacing Carlos Fávaro, who stepped down to run for Senate re-election in Mato Grosso. De Paula emphasized the agrobusiness sector’s importance to Brazil’s economy, accounting for 25% of GDP, employing over 28 million, and contributing to half of national exports. His appointment is part of Lula’s ministerial reform, with 20 ministers expected to leave office to contest the 2026 elections.

item.title: TCU investiga participação de servidores públicos em desfile de Lula
item.description: The Federal Court of Accounts (TCU) has opened an investigation into alleged improper use of public resources by the Planalto Palace in the samba school Acadêmicos de Niterói's parade, which honored President Lula at the Marques da Sapucaí. The court has ordered the Civil House and the Presidency's Secretariat of Communication to provide clarifications by March 24, under suspicion that public servants were used for political-electoral purposes.

item.title: Marina deixa ministério e fala em convites de partidos para eleições
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva announced her resignation from the Ministry of Environment and Climate Change, stating she has received invitations from multiple parties — including PT, PCdoB, PV, PSB, PSOL, PSD and others — to run in the 2026 elections, though she has not yet decided whether to run or for which office. Her departure comes amid disagreements within the federal government over oil exploration in the Equatorial Margin, a contentious issue involving five sedimentary basins in northern and northeastern Brazil.

item.title: O que muda com o aumento da licença-paternidade no Brasil
item.description: Brazil has approved a gradual extension of paternity leave, increasing it from 5 to 20 paid days by 2029, making it the longest in the Americas. The law, signed by President Lula, covers informal workers and adoption cases, with benefits suspended in cases of domestic violence. It aims to promote shared responsibility in childcare and reduce gender inequality in domestic labor.

item.title: Áudio: Chega ao Senado indicação de Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: The Senate received on Wednesday (1st) the presidential message with the nomination of Jorge Messias as a minister of the Federal Supreme Court (STF), filling the vacancy left by the retirement of Luís Roberto Barroso. The nomination was formalized through MSF 7/2026.

item.title: Leite defende consenso após anúncio de Caiado ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) advocated for political consensus after the PSD selected Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) as its presidential pre-candidate. Leite acknowledged policy and stylistic differences with Caiado but emphasized mutual respect and shared ground, arguing dialogue is key to overcoming Brazil’s polarization. The decision followed the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD), with Leite and Caiado statistically tied in voter intention polls (3% vs 4%) per a March 2026 Quaest survey.

item.title: Secretária de Tarcísio defende projetos de longo prazo: ‘Nem no segundo mandato vamos ver’
item.description: São Paulo's Secretary of Environment, Infrastructure, and Logistics, Natália Resende, stated that the state government is prioritizing long-term projects — including the São Paulo-Campinas Intercity Train, the Santos-Guarujá Tunnel, and Sabesp’s investment plan — whose completion may extend beyond a potential second term of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas. In 2025, Sabesp invested R$15.2 billion in sanitation, a 120% increase from the previous year, marking a surge from annual investments of R$4–5 billion to R$15–20 billion.

item.title: “O Ciro é um destemperado”, diz Lula
item.description: Lula stated that former minister Ciro Gomes never reached the presidency due to the electorate's decision, not his own. He noted that Ciro dreamed of being a presidential candidate, ran multiple times, and lost — just as Lula himself has experienced in past elections.

item.title: “Se chegar na minha mão, com oito dias eu vou ler a mensagem na CCJ”, diz Otto Alencar sobre indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: Senator Otto Alencar (PSD-BA), president of the Senate’s Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), said he will read the nomination message for Jorge Messias to the STF within eight days of receiving it and then schedule the confirmation hearing. He has not yet spoken with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre about a timeline but plans to meet with him next week. The presidential nomination was received by the Senate on Wednesday (1st) and awaits referral by Alcolumbre to the CCJ, after which Messias will face a hearing and a full Senate vote.

item.title: Governo inaugura alojamento estudantil do ITA Ceará
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Education Minister Camilo Santana inaugurated the student housing at the new ITA Ceará campus in Fortaleza. The campus, developed through cooperation between the Education and Defense Ministries and the Ceará state government, will offer energy and systems engineering programs to decentralize high-level engineering education and expand access to quality higher education for students from all social backgrounds.

item.title: Marina deixa Ministério do Meio Ambiente sem definir se será candidata em São Paulo e se fica na Rede
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva resigned to run in the 2026 São Paulo elections, without yet deciding whether to seek a Senate seat or run as vice on Fernando Haddad's gubernatorial ticket. She said she remains available to 'fulfill her mission' and is awaiting a court decision in her internal party dispute with Heloísa Helena's faction in Rede Sustentabilidade. Her legacy includes reduced deforestation, restored environmental enforcement, and hosting COP30 in Brazil, despite failing to establish the National Climate Security Authority and losing the battle over oil exploration in the Amazon Mouth, where Ibama granted Petrobras a drilling permit off Amapá.

item.title: Quais os próximos passos após indicação de Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF by Lula requires Senate approval, involving a hearing in the CCJ and a secret vote in the plenary, needing an absolute majority of 41 senators. Despite historical support for presidential nominees, there are signs of resistance, with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre possibly delaying the process and opposition led by Flávio Bolsonaro aiming to reject the nomination. If approved, Messias will be published in the DOU and sworn in at the STF in a ceremony with the Three Powers.

item.title: No Ceará, Lula defende candidatura de Elmano e diz que Camilo é uma 'liderança que precisa ser usada em nível nacional'
item.description: Lula defended Elmano de Freitas' re-election bid in Ceará and stated that Camilo Santana, senator and former Education Minister, should be used as a national campaign asset due to his increased mobility after leaving office. He said Camilo has 'other flights to make' and could be called to run for office in the second half of the year if needed, without specifying the position. The remarks come amid a Datafolha poll showing Ciro Gomes leading gubernatorial intent in Ceará with 47%, followed by Elmano at 32%.

item.title: Planalto envia ao Senado mensagem de indicação de Jorge Messias para vaga no STF
item.description: The Planalto Palace sent the presidential message to the Senate nominating Attorney General Jorge Messias for a STF vacancy, four months after Lula’s formal announcement. Despite initial resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre and bolsonarista senators, JOTA analysts report Messias has sufficient support for approval, aided by evangelical blocs and STF ministers like André Mendonça. Approval requires an absolute majority (41 votes) and is expected to pass by a narrow margin, akin to Paulo Gonet’s reappointment.

item.title: Leite descarta retornar ao PSDB
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) ruled out any return to the PSDB after criticizing the party's decision to nominate former Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) as a presidential pre-candidate. Leite had been invited to rejoin the PSDB to run for the Planalto Palace but rejected the offer, stating there is no room for a party switch.

item.title: O 'bate-volta' de João Campos em Brasília às vésperas de renunciar à Prefeitura do Recife
item.description: Pernambuco gubernatorial pre-candidate João Campos will resign as mayor of Recife tomorrow to run in the October elections. Before stepping down, he is completing a series of deliverables from his second term and will make a 'round-trip' to Brasília this Wednesday. The exclusive article details his final days in municipal office and is available only to subscribers.

item.title: Antigo desafeto de Bolsonaro, Doria chama Flavio para evento do Lide
item.description: Former São Paulo governor João Doria, a longtime adversary of Jair Bolsonaro, invited Bolsonaro’s son, Flávio Bolsonaro, to participate in a Lide group debate in New York on May 12, 2026. The event signals a unexpected political rapprochement between former opponents, despite their ideological differences.

item.title: OAB pede que Moraes bloqueie uso de dados de Wassef em novo inquérito
item.description: The Brazilian Bar Association (OAB) filed a petition with the Supreme Federal Court (STF) requesting that Minister Alexandre de Moraes block the opening of a new investigation based on data extracted from the cellphones of lawyer Frederick Wassef, seized in August 2023. The OAB argues that the nearly three-year gap between seizure and analysis undermines the legal concept of 'fortuitous encounter,' constituting an unconstitutional 'fishing expedition.' The association also requests that, if the new proceeding is authorized, Wassef’s defense be granted unrestricted access to the documented material, as mandated by Binding Precedent 14.

item.title: Messias, indicado ao STF, ajuda Lula em ponte com evangélicos e se diz ‘terrivelmente pacificador’
item.description: Jorge Rodrigo Araújo Messias, nominated by Lula for the STF, is portrayed as loyal, mature, and a 'terribly pacifier', with strong ties to the union movement and the PT-linked Prerrogativas group. His appointment aims to strengthen ties with evangelicals and avoid political risks, following past disappointments with other presidential STF nominations. Despite ignoring an AGU alert about illegal deductions in benefits linked to Lula’s brother, he retains the president’s trust due to his trajectory of gratitude and moderation.

item.title: Carlos deixa a casa de Bolsonaro reclamando de dividir o tempo das visitas entre os irmãos
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro complained about visitation restrictions for his father, Jair Bolsonaro, under humanitarian house arrest, saying he must share 2-hour visits on Wednesdays and Saturdays with his siblings. Minister Alexandre de Moraes denied the request for 'free access' to non-resident children, maintaining the closed regime despite the health-based transfer to home detention.

item.title: Presidência da República oficializa indicação de Jorge Messias ao STF
item.description: The Presidency of the Republic formally submitted the nomination of Jorge Messias, Attorney General of the Union, to the Supreme Federal Court (STF) to fill the vacancy left by the retirement of Minister Luís Roberto Barroso in October 2025. The documentation has been sent to the Federal Senate, which will now review the nomination through a hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) and a plenary vote. Messias, aged 45, could serve on the Court until age 75, potentially for a 30-year term.

item.title: MEC Livros e Idiomas: Lula anuncia aplicativos gratuitos
item.description: The Ministry of Education launched two free apps: MEC Livros, offering 8,000 books for 14-day renewable loans, and MEC Idiomas, providing free English and Spanish lessons. Both are accessible via gov.br account on mobile devices, aiming to promote reading and language learning.

item.title: TCU investiga participação de servidores públicos federais em desfile que homenageou Lula na Sapucaí
item.description: The Federal Court of Accounts (TCU) is investigating whether federal public servants were improperly used in organizing the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba school parade, which honored President Lula during the 2026 Carnival. The TCU has requested the Civil House and Secom to provide details on employee involvement and associated costs, including travel, lodging, and overtime. First Lady Janja withdrew from participation due to fears of retaliation against the school, being replaced by singer Fafá de Belém, while Lula watched the parade from a box provided by Rio Mayor Eduardo Paes.

item.title: A força-tarefa de time de Lula para convencer Alcolumbre a pautar indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: Lula's allies are working behind the scenes to convince Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to advance the nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Court. The administration believes the political climate is favorable and wants to prevent Alcolumbre from delaying the process, which could further strain their already tense relationship. Outreach is expected next week, after the party window closes and congressional activity resumes.

item.title: Lula também esculhamba o STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the Supreme Federal Court (STF) for blocking the continuation of the INSS CPMI, accusing the court of practicing 'eternal investigations'. He pointed out that the STF itself maintains the largest eternal inquiry in Brazilian history since 2019, suggesting hypocrisy in the court's decisions. Lula stated that the greatest attacks on the STF's integrity come from within the tribunal itself.

item.title: PF prende suspeito de vazar dados de ministro do Supremo
item.description: The Federal Police executed one preventive arrest warrant and six search warrants in operation Exfil, investigating the leak and sale of confidential data from Supreme Court ministers, their families, and the Attorney General. The probe alleges illegal access to Federal Revenue systems by public servants, with data being sold for profit. The case is linked to the 2019 fake news inquiry, still under seal, and includes measures such as electronic monitoring, removal from office, and travel bans.

item.title: Os primeiros diagnósticos da economia brasileira para Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro does not yet have a defined economic team, but two economists are conducting initial diagnoses of the Brazilian economy to prepare a plan to be presented by the Zero Um institute at some point. The analysis aims to support future policy proposals, though details remain restricted to subscribers. The work is in a preparatory phase, with no confirmed date for disclosure.

item.title: Duda Salabert deixa PDT e retorna ao PSOL
item.description: Federal deputy Duda Salabert announced her departure from the PDT and return to the PSOL to run for re-election in 2026, citing greater ideological alignment and a broader left-wing project. The move was made four days before the end of the party-switching window (April 3), as permitted by the TSE. Salabert, the first transgender federal deputy from Minas Gerais, had left the PSOL in 2019 over disagreements and accusations of structural transphobia.

item.title: Presidente da CPMI do INSS muda para PSD de Gilberto Kassab
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana, president of the INSS CPMI, will leave Podemos to join Gilberto Kassab’s PSD, with a ceremony in Brasília this Wednesday. He is running for reelection in 2026 against PT’s Marília Campos, amid controversy over alleged data leaks in the CPMI and the rejection of its final report by the Supreme Court. Zema’s vice governor, Matheus Simões, will attend the event and is eyed for Minas Gerais governor, though he polls at only 6.2%.

item.title: A conta da guerra: por que Lula pode pagar o preço nas urnas
item.description: The Iran-related international crisis is pressuring global oil prices, particularly due to the risk of closing the Strait of Hormuz, which could raise fuel costs in Brazil and worsen inflation. President Lula is attempting to distance himself from the crisis by emphasizing national sovereignty, claiming 'the war is not the Brazilian people’s war,' but historical trends suggest incumbent governments bear the political cost of economic downturns regardless of cause. The effectiveness of this strategy is uncertain, as skeptical voters may not accept the narrative, and electoral decisions are likely driven by personal financial impact rather than geopolitical connections.

item.title: Lula formaliza ao Senado a indicação de Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva formally submitted to the Senate the nomination of Attorney-General of the Union Jorge Messias for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court, after over four months of waiting. Messias, 45, a career AGU lawyer and former deputy chief of staff in Dilma Rousseff’s government, needs 41 Senate votes for approval following a hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee. His nomination is seen as a PT victory, given his long-standing ties to Lula and his technical profile, although allies note his religious convictions do not interfere with his judicial decisions.

item.title: A dupla jogada de Kassab para conquistar apoio dos evangélicos e fechar a porta das igrejas para Lula
item.description: Bishop Samuel Ferreira, leader of the Assembly of God, declared support for Ronaldo Caiado’s (PSD) 2026 presidential pre-candidacy, strengthening the alliance between the evangelical segment and Kassab’s party. This move undermines the PT’s hopes of gaining neutrality or support from religious leaders, especially after Ferreira’s friendly meeting with the Lula government in October 2024. Ferreira’s influence over one of Brazil’s largest Protestant denominations signals an early alignment of the evangelical base with Caiado, weakening potential PT outreach in this key voter group.

item.title: Quais os próximos passos após indicação de Jorge Messias para a vaga de Barroso no STF?
item.description: Jorge Messias's nomination to the STF requires Senate approval, including a hearing in the CCJ and a secret floor vote needing at least 41 of 81 senators. Despite historical precedent favoring presidential nominees, Lula's support in the Senate is weakening, with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre potentially delaying the vote and opposition led by Flávio Bolsonaro working to block the nomination.

item.title: Pesquisa: para brasileiros, corrupção, saúde e segurança pública pioraram com Lula no governo
item.description: The Paraná Pesquisas survey shows that 46.3% of Brazilians believe public security worsened under Lula's government, 36.4% see deterioration in health, and 47.3% perceive an increase in corruption. Negative assessments also extend to financial situation (35%) and conditions of the poorest (33%). The margin of error is 2.2 percentage points with 95% confidence, based on 2,080 interviews conducted between March 25 and 28, 2026.

item.title: Aéreas dizem que alta do querosene de aviação vai frear expansão dos voos
item.description: The increase in aviation fuel (QAV) by Petrobras, with a cumulative adjustment of up to 65.7% (56.3% effective this Wednesday plus 9.4% from March), will raise fuel costs to 45% of airlines' operational expenses, according to Abear. This is expected to brake flight expansion, increase airfares by up to 20%, and restrict national connectivity, despite record passenger volumes in Brazil. Abear urges mitigation mechanisms, advocating that Petrobras act as a 'fortress' for aviation by avoiding full pass-through of international price hikes.

item.title: Bastidores: Casos Master e INSS obrigaram Lula a correr por mais um aliado no STF
item.description: After a four-month delay, Lula submitted to the Senate the nomination of Jorge Messias, current minister of the Attorney General’s Office, to the STF, pressured by sensitive investigations such as the Master case and the INSS scandal. The goal is to secure an ally on the Court before potential plea bargains could further complicate the political landscape. Although initially blocked by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, recent signals from MDB and PSD suggest the hostile climate has eased.

item.title: Planalto formaliza indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: The Special Secretariat for Legal Affairs of the Presidency’s Civil House has completed and delivered the documentation for Jorge Messias, minister of the AGU, to be nominated to the STF, officially starting the Senate’s review process. The vacancy, open for four months following Roberto Barroso’s departure, requires 41 votes for approval, but Messias currently has only 25 declared senatorial supports, signaling significant hurdles in both the Constitution and Justice Committee and the full plenary. Senate President Davi Alcolumbre will set the date for the hearing, and the appointment of a new AGU minister will await the outcome of Messias’ confirmation.

item.title: Em vídeo, Silvio Almeida fala pela primeira vez após denúncia da PGR: ‘Sou um homem inocente’
item.description: Former Human Rights Minister Silvio Almeida declared himself innocent in an Instagram video, following a PGR indictment for sexual importuning against Minister Anielle Franco and professor Isabel Rodrigues. He said he remained silent out of respect for his family and the law, and will only speak in court where he can truly defend himself. Almeida denounced the process as violent and unjust, claiming he was fired within 24 hours without due process and publicly labeled a harasser based on irresponsible allegations.

item.title: Governo formaliza ao Senado indicação de Messsias para vaga no STF
item.description: The Lula government formally submitted the nomination of Minister Jorge Messias for a vacancy on the STF (Supreme Federal Court) to the Senate for approval. The appointment now depends on Senate confirmation, as required by the Brazilian Constitution. The move comes amid ongoing debates about judicial independence and the balance of power among Brazil’s branches of government.

item.title: O que revelam os números da pesquisa AtlasIntel sobre Flávio x Lula em Minas
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll in Minas Gerais shows Lula at 47.3% and Flávio Bolsonaro at 46.9% in a potential runoff, mirroring national polarization. The state acts as a barometer of national electoral sentiment, making it pivotal in determining the presidential outcome. Rodrigo Pacheco’s possible gubernatorial candidacy is seen as a strategic move by Lula to strengthen his position in Minas, though the race remains highly competitive and undefined.

item.title: Governadora do Distrito Federal pede ajuda ao governo Lula para crise no BRB
item.description: The governor of the Federal District, Celina Leão (PP), requested assistance from Lula's government to address the financial crisis of BRB (Bank of Brasília), in a phone call with Finance Minister Dario Durigan last Monday (30). The request reflects concerns over the stability of the DF's public financial institution.

item.title: Lula formaliza indicação de Messias ao STF; Senado vai tomar decisão final
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva formally nominated Jorge Messias, head of the Advocacia-Geral da União, for a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court (STF). The nomination was sent to the Senate on Wednesday (1st), over four months after the initial announcement in November 2023. Messias, 46, is an evangelical deacon of the Baptist Church and gained national prominence in 2016 due to his involvement in the recorded audio between Dilma Rousseff and Lula, which led to the nickname 'Bessias' until 2023.

item.title: Os múltiplos papéis de Alckmin nas eleições
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin has been confirmed on Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's 2026 ticket, strengthening outreach to centrist voters and recognizing his performance in office, including reversing U.S. tariffs on Brazilian goods. He will also assist Fernando Haddad in his São Paulo gubernatorial campaign, leveraging his strong presence in the state's interior. Haddad showed strong early polling after announcing his run, raising hopes among PT supporters of defeating incumbent governor Tarcísio de Freitas.

item.title: Após meses de espera, Lula oficializa indicação de Jorge Messias ao STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva formally nominated Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court (STF), following months of delay after Justice Luís Roberto Barroso's retirement. Messias, the Union's Attorney General and an evangelical Baptist, must undergo Senate confirmation, a process currently hindered by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who initially opposed the nomination and attempted to rush the hearing. If confirmed, Messias will join the STF's Second Chamber, representing the first Supreme Court nomination since the 19th century not to face senatorial rejection.
